elections.note: Certified final = 669,627 ballots (Election Day 298,112 + VBM 371,515) from CA SoS 2012 Voter Participation Statistics by County, primary. Election-night plateau not sourceable via Wayback: Riverside's Sequoia 'eresults' live page (voteinfo.net/Elections/20121106/eresults/) was not archived on/near election night, and Riverside used no Dominion ElectionSummaryReport before 2019. WebFetch blocked; curl+CDX+headless-Chrome used. Re-verified 2026-06: Riverside's /Elections/<date>/docs/ press-release archive holds only operational releases -- no morning-after 'semi-official results' release. DEAD-END NOTE EXTENSION (2026-07-09, Task 9): rivco.org does not run CivicPlus DocumentCenter (0 captures); @RivCoVote and plausible alternate handles have zero Wayback captures. RECOVERY (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank Press-Enterprise): two Nov 8, 2012 Press-Enterprise articles independently state the same countywide remaining-ballot figure. 'Buster facing defeat' (docref news/14270915258D4BE8): 'Unofficial final results from the county Registrar of Voters show Kevin Jeffries leading Buster ... with all precincts reporting. But about 183,000 vote-by-mail, provisional and damaged ballots remained to be counted.' Also states Buster 'didn't concede Wednesday, Nov. 7' (the count had not moved as of Wednesday). 'Bono Mack, Baca ousters hit clout' (docref news/14270914E8F945E8): 'With all precincts reporting ... Bono Mack said she prefers to await the final tally of 183,000 mail-in, provisional and damaged ballots that remained to be counted countywide ... The Riverside County voter registrar's office is expected to issue an updated vote count at 6 p.m. Thursday, Nov. 8.' The registrar's own posting schedule (next update explicitly Thursday 6 p.m., none between election night and the Wednesday print deadline) brackets this as the held election-night plateau (San Diego 2012 pattern: RUNBOOK 8 'county's own posting schedule bracketing the report'). PLATEAU = certified final 669,627 minus 183,000 outstanding = 486,627 counted. 486,627/669,627 = 72.67%, within the ~55-75% precinct-era calibration band (high end; Riverside's 'slow counter' reputation concerns the multi-day VBM canvass tail after this point, not the initial precincts-plus-early-VBM tranche). Not the 8 p.m. first tranche (would be far lower, before any all-precincts-reporting milestone). Comparable true. Confidence secondary: news report of a registrar-derived, not directly stated, ballots-counted figure.

Certified final = 357,764 ballots (Election Day 124,829 + VBM 232,935) from CA SoS 2014 complete Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics page, primary; registration 891,575, final turnout 40.13%. Wayback/press-release/DocumentCenter/social-handle routes exhausted (no election-night eresults capture; no registrar semi-final release -- see prior dead-end history). RECOVERY (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank Press-Enterprise): 'Council members succeed, struggle' (Nov 6 2014, docref news/151717B89F05D948): 'The Riverside County Registrar of Voters office announced that 93,000 votes are still to be processed, including 66,000 vote-by-mail ballots, 19,000 provisional ballots and 8,000 damaged ballots. The next update will be posted at 6 p.m. tonight' (same Riverside pattern as 2012: held since election night, first post-night update two days later). Independent cross-check, 'a new low in area's Voter turnout?' (same date, docref news/151717B8F7250120): 'In Riverside County, turnout was 29.8 percent as of Wednesday. It likely will end up at about 39 or 40 percent once all the ballots are counted, said Registrar Rebecca Spencer' -- Spencer's '39 or 40 percent' estimate matches the eventual certified 40.13% almost exactly, validating the article; 29.8% x 891,575 registered = 265,689, within 0.3% of the 93,000-derived 264,764 (independent method, second bylined article). PLATEAU = 357,764 minus 93,000 outstanding = 264,764. 264,764/357,764 = 74.01%, high end of the ~55-75% precinct-era band but corroborated two independent ways. Comparable true. Confidence secondary (news-derived registrar figure).

Certified final = 769,193 ballots (Election Day 264,638 + VBM 504,555) from CA SoS 2016 Voter Participation Statistics by County, primary. Wayback/press-release/DocumentCenter/social-handle routes exhausted (earliest Sequoia eresults capture is a 2016-11-12 post-canvass 'Last Updated November 11, 2016 4:54 PM' showing 532,023, a later canvass update NOT the plateau; see prior dead-end history). RECOVERY (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank Press-Enterprise): two Nov 10 2016 local-race articles carry identical registrar boilerplate -- 'One Eastvale council race especially close' (docref news/16095824826D46E0) and 'Corona voters back council districts, pick new treasurer' (docref news/160958246D11FAA8): 'The figures, posted by the Riverside County Registrar of Voters Office early Wednesday, are not final. Officials have yet to count 262,000 provisional, damaged and vote-by-mail ballots submitted countywide.' A third, differently-bylined Nov 10 article ('Familiar faces on southwest councils', docref news/16095823F5443EE8) corroborates ('more than 200,000 ballots need to be counted') and states 'The next update from the registrar's office is set for 6 p.m. Friday' -- bracketing the Wednesday-posted count as unchanged through Thursday (RUNBOOK 8 posting-schedule leg). The subsequent Friday update ('Latest vote count: No big changes', Nov 12, docref news/160A0028EF5A1DB8) shows 136,000+67,000+9,000=212,000 remaining, a lower later-canvass figure consistent in direction/magnitude with 262,000 minus ~2 days of processing (confirms 262,000 was the earlier held state, not itself post-canvass). PLATEAU = 769,193 minus 262,000 outstanding = 507,193. 507,193/769,193 = 65.94%, closely matching SF's own 2016 calibration (66%). Comparable true. Confidence secondary (news-derived registrar figure).

Certified final = 650,545 ballots (Election Day 194,606 + VBM 455,939) from CA SoS 2018 Voter Participation Statistics by County; corroborated by Riverside Summary_Final.pdf (Run Date/Time 12/7/18). Primary. Wayback/press-release/DocumentCenter/social-handle routes exhausted (earliest eresults capture is 2018-11-28 post-certification; see prior dead-end history). RECOVERY (2026-07-09, Task 7, NewsBank Press-Enterprise): 'Elections - Many Inland ballots still not processed or counted' (docref news/16F9051D0AED5530): 'Final unofficial vote counts aren't expected to be released until 6 p.m. today for Riverside County ... Riverside County Registrar of Voters Rebecca Spencer said she had a crew of about 300 workers processing ballots overnight Tuesday. According to Wednesday vote totals, they counted about 300,000 ballots. On Wednesday morning, Spencer's staff started going over about 200,000 vote-by-mail, 45,000 provisional and 5,000 damaged ballots.' Spencer is directly quoted the same Wednesday: 'We're not counting anything today ... We are sorting out all the materials from the polling places' -- confirming the 300,000 figure was frozen (no counting that day) at the time of the report, a self-confirming plateau. PLATEAU = 300,000 (directly stated, not a subtraction derivation). 300,000/650,545 = 46.12%. Below the general ~55-75% precinct-era band but explicitly within the brief's 'mid-40s-to-60s on-night shares are plausible' carve-out for Riverside's notoriously slow 2010s counting (this election also saw unusually high turnout and long lines per the same article, plausibly slowing the count further). Comparable true. Confidence secondary (news-quoted registrar figure, round number).

Election-night plateau = 205,813 ballots ('Voters Cast: 205,813 of 1,310,928 (15.70%)'; Governor Times Cast 205,813) from the official Riverside Dominion EMS 'Semi-Official Election Results' report, Last-Modified 11/09/2022 02:04 PST (the ~2 a.m. final election-night report, within the hourly-until-3am window). This held as the live page until the first canvass update (300,498, Last-Modified 11/11/2022) -- i.e. it plateaued through the next day before the multi-day canvass resumed, matching the metric. Corroborated by Idyllwild Town Crier (Nov 9, 2022): election-night counting complete at ~205,000 with ~335,000 ballots remaining. 205,813/604,617 = 34.0%. Certified final 604,617 (Election Day 59,812 + VBM 544,805) from CA SoS, primary. snapshot 20221109110809 used. First epollbook general.

elections.flag: election-night-stage mismatch: 611,101 is the Nov-6 17:35 first daily canvass update, NOT the ~3 a.m. election-night plateau (which is unarchived); it overstates the true plateau and is not stage-comparable to the clean 2022 2 a.m. figure -- exclude from the pre/post epollbook comparison.
elections.note: 611,101 'Ballots Counted' from the earliest Wayback capture of Riverside's livevoterturnout ENR, embedded timestamp 'Updated: 11/6/2024 5:35:21 PM' -- the FIRST daily canvass update (afternoon of the day after the Nov 5 election), NOT the ~3 a.m. election-night plateau. Riverside reports hourly until ~3 a.m. (confirmed by the registrar's own 'November 5 Election Night Results Reporting' release, rivco.org, dated Nov 4, 2024: 'results will continue to be uploaded every hour until approximately 3 a.m.') then updates daily (excl. Sundays). 611,101/959,098 = 63.7% slightly OVER-states the election-night share and is NOT strictly stage-comparable to the clean 2 a.m. 2022 figure. Next captures: 685,126 (11/9), 912,011 (11/15). Certified final 959,098 (Election Day 170,437 + VBM 788,661) from CA SoS, primary. snapshot 20241107034053 used. Post-epollbook, pre-ASV. Re-verified 2026-06: the only Wayback ENR captures are 20241107034053 (the 11/6 17:35 update shown above) and 20241109 -- there is NO capture in the poll-close-to-3 a.m. window; Riverside posted no 2024 Dominion EMS ElectionSummaryReport to voteinfo.net/Elections/20241105/docs/ (none archived), issued no morning-after 'semi-official results' release stating an election-night total, and local news (Riverside Record, Raincross Gazette) state precincts-reporting but no countywide election-night ballot count. The ~3 a.m. plateau is therefore genuinely unarchived; 611,101 is retained as the nearest datapoint (an upper bound) and flagged not-comparable per comparable:false above.
